dependency_choices.reason: Native Rust Starlark parser and evaluator from starlark-rust.
Registers the prototype regex helper as a Starlark global.
Small error adapter for fail-closed policy evaluation paths.

request_shape.name: optional stable policy id
request_shape.source: Starlark source defining evaluate(ctx), used when policy.source is absent

return_shape.model: optional model when action is switch_model

trace.span: optional, not implemented in this spike

covered_scenarios: current-session history regex count block through regex_match(pattern, text)
structured guard retry on json_syntax guard events
fail closed on invalid or missing policy action results
fail closed on visible runaway while policy because Standard dialect rejects while
fail closed on range(...) loop attempts before evaluation
fail closed on unavailable filesystem and network host capability attempts

checks.notes: Required one-time mise trust for this repo config, then ran cargo fmt --check and cargo test. 26 tests passed.

latency_observations.unit_test_wall_time: cargo test completed the Rust test binary in about 0.26s after compilation
latency_observations.expected_runtime_shape: Each request parses, compiles, and evaluates the policy source in-process. This is deterministic and acceptable for a spike but should be cached or precompiled before production use.

safety_observations: The evaluator exposes only standard Starlark globals plus regex_match; no filesystem, process, or network functions are registered.
load/import attempts fail closed because no loader is configured.
Policy errors, parse failures, invalid action enums, missing switch_model target, and serialization problems return action=block.
Returned error reasons are shortened to avoid leaking host paths or internals.
limitations: No AST/source-span trace metadata is emitted yet; traces use stable policy/rule ids.

The in-process starlark-rust API did not expose a production-grade instruction timeout/fuel hook in the timebox. A statement hook was wired, but tight range loops required a conservative range(...) preflight block.
The prototype should not run untrusted arbitrary policy source in production without a stronger execution budget or an isolated worker/process boundary.
Policy source is parsed and evaluated per call rather than cached.
Regex support is provided as a small Rust global helper instead of a full policy standard library.

viability_verdict.summary: Native Rust Starlark is viable for deterministic policy semantics, JSON-like ABI handling, fail-closed result validation, and sandboxed host capability shape. Production viability depends on adding robust execution budgeting or moving evaluation behind a killable process boundary, plus policy compilation caching and source-span tracing.

findings.finding: The first commit only blocked large literal range(...) calls, which left dynamic range(n) policies able to run in-process too long.
findings.resolution: Follow-up fix fail-closes all range(...) use in this spike surface because the required ABI examples do not need range and fail-closed behavior is the priority.

findings.finding: The endpoint parses and evaluates policy source per request and runs in-process.
findings.resolution: Recorded as a limitation; production should cache compiled policies and use a stronger instruction budget or killable worker boundary.

findings.finding: Traces carry stable policy/rule ids and reasons but no source spans.
findings.resolution: Recorded as a limitation because core fail-closed behavior was prioritized.

findings.finding: Native tests cover the required behavior and invalid-result negative paths, but there is no HTTP-level test for the prototype endpoint.
findings.resolution: Accepted for spike scope because the deterministic callable evaluator is directly tested.
